methods.method_name: Bee Colony Acclimation and Foraging Training
methods.description: Process for acclimating a Bombus impatiens colony and training bees to forage in a controlled environment.

steps.description: The colony was allowed to acclimate until new foragers began to eclose 23 days later.
steps.searchable_summary: allow bee colony to acclimate until new foragers emerge

steps.description: Bees were trained to forage for pollen and nectar in a foraging arena.
steps.searchable_summary: train bees to forage in a controlled arena

methods.description: Setup of a controlled foraging environment for observing bee foraging behavior.

steps.description: An arena constructed of plywood was divided into two equal-sized foraging chambers.
steps.searchable_summary: construct and divide foraging arena into chambers

steps.description: The arena had a clear acrylic ceiling and was lit from above by fluorescent lights.
steps.searchable_summary: install lighting and ceiling in foraging arena

steps.description: The colony was kept dark while lights in the arenas were set to a 12:12 light:dark cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: set light cycle for foraging arena

steps.description: RFID readers were mounted at the entrance of each foraging chamber.
steps.searchable_summary: install RFID readers at chamber entrances

methods.description: Procedure for tagging bees with RFID transponders to track their foraging behavior.

steps.description: Unlabelled bees that left the colony box were tagged with RFID transponders.
steps.searchable_summary: tag unlabelled bees with RFID transponders

steps.description: Unlabelled foragers were trapped and tagged with a single tag glued to their thoraxes.
steps.searchable_summary: trap and tag foragers with RFID on thorax

steps.description: Bees were placed into vials with pollen scent to calm them before returning to the colony.
steps.searchable_summary: calm tagged bees with pollen scent before colony return
